title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary William Galvin agreed to complete reports on voting rights for incarcerated individuals after a lawsuit prompted action.
Galvin's office cited technical issues for delays in mandated post-election reports, required by the 2022 VOTES Act.
Nearly 9,000 eligible incarcerated voters in Massachusetts were affected, with improvements promised before September's primary election.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam has signed a three-year contract with the New England Patriots, transitioning from the Buffalo Bills.
Gilliam sees similarities between Patriots quarterback Drake Maye and former NFL MVP Josh Allen of the Buffalo Bills.
Gilliam aims to enhance the Patriots' running game, leveraging his physical fullback style for short-yardage situations.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez will miss at least two months of the 2026 season after shoulder surgery.
Boston Red Sox signed veteran lefty Danny Coulombe to a one-year deal to strengthen bullpen.
Coulombe played for Twins and Rangers in 2025, posting a 2.30 ERA over 55 appearances.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, a Bishop Fenwick High School senior, is charged with murdering Janet Swallow in her Danvers home.
Swallow, 68, was found with fatal wounds, and the attack is believed to be random, per Essex County DA Paul Tucker.
DeMayo was apprehended in Lynn with a bloody knife; no known connection exists between him and Swallow.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are seeking a suspect who allegedly assaulted a pregnant woman at a laundromat on March 1.
The suspect is described as a 6’0” light-skinned male wearing a Yankees hat, beige hoodie, and blue pants.
Detectives are looking for a woman present during the assault, described wearing a gray jacket and black sweatpants.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer by flashing blue lights.
Beltran, a former Lawrence police officer, was on administrative leave in September 2024 and fired in March 2025.
He was previously suspended from the Massachusetts Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission, losing his certification.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots are interested in acquiring Eagles wideout A.J. Brown to enhance their offense.
Eagles initially demand a 2027 first-round pick and a 2026 second-round pick for Brown.
NFL insider believes Eagles may lower their asking price, enabling a potential Patriots deal.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck fire on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough caused a shutdown of westbound lanes Thursday morning.
The fire occurred near mile marker 102.4 and was extinguished by 8:30 a.m. with assistance from Grafton firefighters.
No injuries were reported; all lanes reopened by 1:30 p.m. as the fire's cause remains under investigation.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Wide receiver Romeo Doubs signed a four-year, $80 million deal with the New England Patriots during NFL free agency.
Doubs chose the Patriots to team up with quarterback Drake Maye and praised the team's winning culture under coach Mike Vrabel.
Formerly with the Green Bay Packers, Doubs caught 55 passes for 724 yards and six touchdowns in 2025.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson with 60,000 miles required a complete engine replacement due to manufacturing defects.
Engine issues in Hyundai/Kia models are linked to improperly machined bearings; similar problems affect Toyota and Chevrolet.
The manufacturer's 100,000-mile warranty covered the engine replacement for the Hyundai Tucson owner.
